title: senecio cymbalarioides var. borealis
intro: Senecio cymbalarioides var. borealis, also known as the boreal ragwort or boreal Jacob's ladder, is a rare and unique plant species found in the mountainous regions of North America. It is a member of the daisy family and is prized for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as respiratory problems, skin conditions, and wounds, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation and promoting healing. The herbal preparations of Senecio cymbalarioides var. borealis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to create topical creams and salves for skin and wound care.
